name: Double Jump
description: An additional jump that can be triggered after leaving the ground.
design_notes: Double jump extends the player's correction window and changes how levels are authored. It can be a forgiving safety tool, a required traversal upgrade, or a combat air-control option. Tuning should define whether it resets vertical momentum, adds to existing momentum, or uses a fixed launch profile.
design_purpose: Let players recover from small jump mistakes without removing the need for platform judgment.
Gate routes and collectibles behind a readable movement upgrade.
Increase air-control expression in combat or hazard navigation.
player_fantasy: The player feels acrobatic and capable of turning a failed jump into a controlled recovery.

parameters.name: extra_jump_count
parameters.description: Number of additional jumps granted before a reset condition.

parameters.name: air_jump_vertical_impulse
parameters.description: Vertical launch applied by the extra jump.

parameters.name: momentum_reset_ratio
parameters.description: Share of downward velocity removed before applying the air jump.

parameters.name: air_jump_buffer_ms
parameters.description: Buffered input accepted before an extra jump becomes available.

edge_cases: Walking off a ledge after a grounded jump must not accidentally consume the extra jump unless the design wants it.
Landing on moving platforms, enemies, water, or one-way platforms must reset jump counters consistently.
Buffered jump input during knockback or ledge grab should not spend an air jump after control returns.
Variable jump height and double jump must agree on whether release cuts the second jump short.
common_bugs: Extra jump resets every frame while touching wall collision because wall contact is treated as ground.
The second jump stacks with downward velocity and feels too weak after falling for a long distance.
Animation state reports grounded early and grants an unintended third jump.
Networked clients disagree about jump count after prediction correction.
balancing_notes: High vertical impulse turns double jump into a route unlock; low impulse makes it mainly a correction tool.
Resetting downward momentum is more forgiving than additive impulse and should be considered in hazard spacing.
accessibility_notes: Combine with jump buffering and coyote time when the game expects frequent precise air jumps.
Use a distinct sound or animation for the spent air jump so players can track remaining jumps without reading UI.

implementation_notes.unity: Use an explicit movement-state enum and feed the final desired velocity into CharacterController.Move or Rigidbody movement from FixedUpdate; keep dash and jump assists outside animation callbacks. For Double Jump, validate this path with parameters such as extra_jump_count and air_jump_vertical_impulse.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep the mechanic state on CharacterBody2D or CharacterBody3D, update velocity in _physics_process, and separate input buffering from collision response. For Double Jump, validate this path with parameters such as extra_jump_count and air_jump_vertical_impulse.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use CharacterMovement with a custom movement state or Gameplay Ability; gate activation through prediction-safe input and reconcile movement corrections cleanly in networked play. For Double Jump, validate this path with parameters such as extra_jump_count and air_jump_vertical_impulse.
implementation_notes.web: Represent the mechanic as deterministic state in the simulation loop, then apply resolved displacement before rendering through Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or a custom engine. For Double Jump, validate this path with parameters such as extra_jump_count and air_jump_vertical_impulse.

example_games.title: Super Metroid
example_games.note: Uses movement upgrades to expand reachable spaces.

example_games.title: Hollow Knight
example_games.note: Uses an air-mobility upgrade to change traversal options.
